title: cassandra angustifolia
intro: Cassandra angustifolia, also known as California buckwheat or wild buckwheat, is a perennial herb native to the western coast of North America, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cassandra angustifolia include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Cassandra angustifolia can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and crushing the leaves to use as a topical poultice.
